hawkish_phrases: The steady erosion of slack has reduced the degree to which it is appropriate for the MPC to accommodate an extended period of inflation above the target
Unemployment has fallen to a 42-year low and the MPC judges that the level of remaining slack is limited
CPI inflation rose to 3.0% in September
the MPC now judges it appropriate to tighten modestly the stance of monetary policy
domestic inflationary pressures gradually pick up as spare capacity is absorbed
dovish_phrases: any future increases in Bank Rate would be expected to be at a gradual pace and to a limited extent
Monetary policy continues to provide significant support to jobs and activity
Consumption growth remains sluggish in the near term
Business investment is being affected by uncertainties around Brexit
inflation is expected to fall back over the next year
summary: The MPC voted 7-2 to increase Bank Rate by 25bp to 0.50%, judging it appropriate to tighten modestly given erosion of slack and inflation above target, but signaling future increases would be gradual and limited.
